content.problemStatement:
You are given a positive integer NNN. Note that NNN may exceed the range of a 64-bit integer.

Find a positive integer MMM (without leading zeroes) divisible by 888, formed by changing **minimum** number of digits in NNN.

Note that the number of digits in MMM must be equal to the number of digits in NNN.

If there are multiple answers, you may print any one of them.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line of input containing two space separated numbers - the length of NNN and NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, a positive number MMM (without leading zeroes) divisible by 888 and formed by changing the **minimum** number of digits of NNN.


If there are multiple answers, you may print any one of them.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: 777 is not divisible by 888. It can be changed to 888 which is divisible by 888 by changing 111 digit which is the minimum possible.
Test case 222: 512512512 is already divisible by 888. So, no need to change it.
Test case 333: 104410441044 is not divisible by 888. It can be changed to 114411441144 which is divisible by 888 by changing 111 digit which is the minimum possible.
Note that other valid answers like 102410241024, 104010401040, 134413441344 are also possible, since each of them require changing a single digit only.
